title: Add minIterations as explicit lower-bound stop guard
summary: Define minIterations as a bounded integer lower stop guard, defaulting to 3 and requiring minIterations <= maxIterations.

title: Model convergenceMode off as semantic switch not threshold hack
summary: Use an explicit convergenceMode enum so convergence-driven STOP can be disabled while hard caps and safety halts remain active.

title: Persist new config fields into YAML-created config and JSONL config records
summary: Carry minIterations and convergenceMode through setup bindings, config template population, and the initial JSONL config record with deterministic legacy defaults.

title: Make minIterations a constrained tunable and keep convergenceMode locked
summary: Add minIterations to optimizer-managed tunables only with minIterations <= maxIterations validation; keep convergenceMode locked because it changes stop semantics.

Neither vendored reference repo exposes fields literally named minIterations or convergenceMode; this pass maps adjacent mechanisms: explicit disabled caps, bounded minimum config, and semantic switches.

The current deep-research template lists only maxIterations, convergenceThreshold, and stuckThreshold as optimizer tunables, so the lower-bound guard and convergence-off mode need new config and governance contracts.
The optimizer currently validates individual manifest fields and ranges, but it has no visible cross-field constraint surface for minIterations <= maxIterations.
